mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive t-statistic for the threshold crossing falls below -1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and consumer credit indicates synchronized demand-supply mismatch that forces margin compression and equity de-rating, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression fails to exceed 0.05 in magnitude over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high and low interaction states.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.5%, an increase in the federal funds rate accelerates discount rate expansion faster than earnings growth can compensate, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold and FEDFUNDS rises concurrently over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rate signals expanding aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between quarterly GDP growth acceleration and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10 across all available data points.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 100, it indicates supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that erode profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean during periods where INDPRO remains below 100 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a labor market cooling cycle that reduces consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha (p > 0.10) in a Fama-MacBeth regression over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market downturn where new housing starts drop below 1.2 million units, construction-related equity valuations contract sharply, dragging down S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative relationship with HOUST declines when the series falls below 1.2 million units over a 3-year backtest.
